skill.name: AI Chat / Text Generation — verging.ai
skill.description: Generate AI chat completions using GPT-4o through the verging.ai proxy API. Supports both streaming (SSE) and non-streaming responses.
skill.summary: Documentation skill for the verging.ai GPT-4o chat API with streaming and non-streaming examples.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill (README.md + SKILL.md) with no executable code shipped. Static analysis flagged curl, npx, and export commands in the README as potential external command execution, but these are documentation examples showing users how to call the verging.ai API. URLs to verging.ai are the documented API endpoints. The VERGING_API_KEY env var is standard API key configuration. No prompt injection attempts, data exfiltration patterns, or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Network requests to external API endpoint
low_findings.description: Documentation references the verging.ai API endpoint at https://verging.ai/api/v1/ai/chat. This is expected for an API-based chat skill and is the documented purpose of the skill.

low_findings.title: API key referenced via environment variable
low_findings.description: VERGING_API_KEY environment variable is used in documentation examples. This is standard practice for API key management and does not represent a security risk in a documentation skill.

low_findings.title: Shell commands shown in documentation
low_findings.description: curl, npx, and export commands appear in README code blocks as usage examples. These are not executable by the skill itself but are instructions for end users.

content.user_title: Generate GPT-4o Chat Completions via verging.ai
content.value_statement: Users need a simple way to call GPT-4o through the verging.ai proxy. This skill provides ready-to-use curl examples for both streaming and non-streaming chat completions.
actual_capabilities: Send non-streaming chat completion requests to GPT-4o via verging.ai API
Send streaming chat completion requests using Server-Sent Events
Configure temperature and max_tokens parameters for response control
Support system, user, and assistant message roles
Receive token usage and credit consumption reports per request
Authenticate requests using the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable
limitations: Requires a minimum balance of 5 credits before calling the API
Only works with the verging.ai proxy endpoint, not direct OpenAI access
No support for function calling or tool use in the documented examples
Documentation is curl-based; integration requires manual HTTP handling

use_cases.title: Quick Chat Completion from Terminal
use_cases.description: Send a single message to GPT-4o and receive a response without writing any application code.

use_cases.title: Streaming AI Responses for Real-Time Apps
use_cases.description: Use the SSE streaming mode to build applications that display AI responses as they are generated.

use_cases.title: Batch Text Generation Tasks
use_cases.description: Integrate verging.ai chat completions into scripts and automation pipelines for content generation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Non-Streaming Chat
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the verging.ai chat skill to send a non-streaming completion request. Ask GPT-4o: 'Explain quantum computing in one paragraph.' Show me the curl command and the expected JSON response format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a simple chat request

prompt_templates.title: Streaming with System Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the verging.ai chat skill to send a streaming request with a system prompt. Set the system role to 'You are a helpful assistant' and stream a response to 'Write a haiku about coding.' Show the SSE event format.

prompt_templates.title: Parameterized Completion
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the verging.ai chat skill to send a request with custom temperature (0.7) and max_tokens (256). Use the model parameter to select gpt-4o explicitly. Display the token usage and credits consumed in the response.

prompt_templates.title: Error Handling and Credits Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the verging.ai chat skill to handle error responses. Show me what happens when credits are insufficient (HTTP 402, error code 40001) and when rate limits are hit (HTTP 429). Include the error response JSON structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building robust error handling for production use

output_examples.input: Non-streaming request asking GPT-4o to explain quantum computing.
output: The skill returns a JSON response with a 'content' field containing the AI-generated explanation.
A 'usage' object reports prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, and total_tokens for the request.
A 'credits_consumed' field indicates how many credits were deducted for the call.

output_examples.input: Streaming request with stream: true and a haiku prompt.
output: The response arrives as Server-Sent Events, each prefixed with 'data:'.
Content chunks are emitted incrementally, for example: data: {"content": "Quantum "}
The final chunk includes usage and credits information, followed by a 'data: [DONE]' sentinel.

output_examples.input: Request when the account has fewer than 5 credits.
output: The API returns HTTP status 402 with error code 40001.
The error body contains a message such as 'Insufficient credits. Required: 5, available: 2'.
The upstream_error field is null since the error originates from the proxy itself.

best_practices: Store your API key in the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable rather than hardcoding it in scripts.
Use streaming mode for long responses to reduce perceived latency in user-facing applications.
Monitor the credits_consumed field in responses to track usage and avoid unexpected depletion.
anti_patterns: Do not commit your VERGING_API_KEY to version control or share it in public repositories.
Do not attempt to call the API without first verifying your account has at least 5 credits available.
Do not ignore rate limit responses (HTTP 429); implement backoff using the X-RateLimit-Reset header.

faq.question: What model does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill targets GPT-4o through the verging.ai proxy API. You can also pass a different model name in the request body.

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Visit verging.ai, log in, click your avatar in the top right, and create a key from the API Keys section.

faq.question: What is the minimum credit balance required?
faq.answer: Your account must have at least 5 credits before you can make a request. Each request consumes a minimum of 1 credit.

faq.question: Can I stream the response?
faq.answer: Yes. Set 'stream': true in the request body to receive Server-Sent Events. Each content chunk arrives as a data-prefixed event.

faq.question: How are credits charged?
faq.answer: Input tokens cost 1 credit per 10K tokens. Output tokens cost 3 credits per 10K tokens. There is a minimum charge of 1 credit per request.

faq.question: What error codes should I handle?
faq.answer: Handle HTTP 402 for insufficient credits, HTTP 429 for rate limits, and HTTP 502/503 for upstream or service errors.
